goal: Implement a governed Settings Center for portal configuration, contextual help, local user management with AD links, memory ACL inheritance and .env proposal workflows.
global_constraints: Keep common settings infrastructure in apps.settings_center and domain semantics in domain descriptor modules.
Do not turn apps.settings_center into the owner of memory, account, AI or workorder business logic.
Default contracts in contracts/ are not edited by production GUI; runtime contracts in data/contracts/ are edited atomically.
Raw secret values must never be stored in contracts, audit payloads, help prompts, logs or repository files.
Use Vaultwarden-style external links for secret handles in the MVP.
Users are managed locally; Active Directory is linked as external identity and ACL/group metadata source.
Memory ACL inheritance must fail closed when ACL metadata or principal mapping cannot be resolved.
.env values are restart-required bootstrap settings; production uses read-only/proposal workflow by default.
GUI is Django templates + HTMX for the first implementation stage.
Temporary artifacts belong in .local/ and runtime artifacts under data/.
cross_cutting_acceptance: Every write path has server-side permission checks and audit.
Every significant setting has a stable setting_id and contextual help metadata.
Masked diffs are used for sensitive values.
Post-apply validation is run before reporting success.
Docs and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml are updated after structural changes.
verification_commands: python manage.py test apps.settings_center.tests apps.accounts.tests apps.memory.tests apps.ai.tests
